questionHtml:
Which equation represents a process in yeast that causes bread to rise?


A.  Glucose + oxygen → ethanol + carbon dioxide

B.  Glucose → carbon dioxide + water

C.  Glucose + oxygen → carbon dioxide + water

D.  Glucose → ethanol + carbon dioxide

meta.title: Which equation represents a process in yeast that causes bread to rise? A. Glucose + oxygen → ethanol + carbon dioxide B. Glucose → carbon dioxide + water C. Glucose + oxygen → car
breadcrumbLabels: C1.2.10. Anaerobic cell respiration in yeast and its use in brewing and baking
